summary:
The core rationale hinges on disease biology—scabies depends entirely upon direct human-to-human skin contact during active infection phases without an asymptomatic carrier state enabling broad preventive coverage. Unlike parasitic worms or vitamin deficiencies involving widespread exposure pathways, scabies cannot be effectively controlled by treating entire communities blindly because infected individuals present only after symptomatic onset, making systematic screening impractical—and thus rendering large-scale medication delivery futile rather than effective.

The summary should end exactly at this sentence.
